key_insight: GeoSD makes self-distillation safer for reasoning by scaling teacher guidance with student overlap and limiting prediction drift in the same probability geometry.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical post-training recipe for LLMs that improves out-of-distribution mathematical reasoning without sacrificing in-distribution gains.
method: Hellinger loss weakens teacher pressure on tokens the student cannot currently support.
A proximal penalty uses Fisher-Rao distance to keep the student near a recent checkpoint.
Natural-gradient updates operate in distribution geometry rather than raw parameter space.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning benchmarks and three model families, GeoSD improves average OOD accuracy by 5.7–8.6 points over the base model at scales from 1.7B to 32B.
watch_item: The approach adds geometric regularization and checkpoint tracking, so implementation needs careful tuning to avoid under-updating or extra training complexity.
